Quitman · Wood County · East Texas

A new kind of memory care,
coming to East Texas.

The Memory Health Life Center will give 54 East Texans living with dementia a place to live with dignity, purpose, and daily life, and train the caregivers our region needs.

Give to Build It See the Vision
A project of
WCHCF.ORG

Pending state match

The Vision

A dementia care campus built around daily life.

Inspired by the Hogeweyk model from the Netherlands and adapted for East Texas families, the Memory Health Life Center is being designed as a residential community, not a hallway. Residents will live in homes, walk the grounds, tend gardens and animals, and share daily life with neighbors, caregivers, and visiting students.

Read the full project overview Learn about the need

54

residents

Residential homes designed for dignity, independence, and daily life.

29

acre campus

Adjacent to UT Health Quitman Hospital, owned by Wood County Central Hospital District.

A regional

training ground

In partnership with UT Tyler School of Medicine and other educators.

Why Memory Care Matters

Rural East Texas families are facing dementia alone.

The need is here. The current options aren't enough. Families travel for hours to reach specialists, caregivers carry years of around-the-clock care without support, and existing facilities too often default to confinement.

459,300

Texans age 65+ living with Alzheimer's

Nearly 1 in 8 Texas seniors — and the number climbs every year. Rural counties feel it first, and longest.

Source · Alzheimer's Association, 2024

≈ 30,000

Unpaid caregivers across our East Texas service area

Sons, daughters, and spouses carrying the weight at home — most without backup, training, or a single hour of relief.

Local estimate · county prevalence × TX caregiver ratios

54

Residential memory-care spaces planned in Quitman

A new model of care for East Texas — residential support paired with caregiver education and workforce training.

In development

What the Center Will Provide

Care that looks like a life, not a hallway.

Homes, a working farm, gardens, classrooms, and the people to make it real, under one roof, in one community.

  • Resident Homes

    54 residential beds in small-household groupings that feel like home, not an institution.

  • Working Farm & Animals

    Daily routines with land and animals. A proven path to purpose and engagement.

  • Gardens & Walking Paths

    Outdoor space designed for safety, sensory engagement, and daily movement.

  • Caregiver & Family Support

    Respite, education, and ongoing support for the families holding it all together.

  • Classrooms & Training

    A teaching site for the next generation of dementia-competent professionals.

  • Brain Health Programs

    Diagnostics, treatment, education, and quality-of-life programming for the region.

Regional Resource

Built in Quitman.
Built for the region.

Adjacent to UT Health Quitman Hospital. A teaching site for the University of Texas at Tyler School of Medicine. Designed to serve East Texas families, including low- and moderate-income families, with a model that prizes daily life over containment.

Become a partner

  • Health Care Partner

    UT Health Quitman Hospital

    Adjacent campus, integrated care pathway

  • Education Partner

    UT Tyler School of Medicine

    Curriculum integration confirmed

  • Public Sector

    City of Quitman

    Mayor Randy Dunn, project champion

  • Site Authority

    Wood County Central Hospital District

    29-acre site adjacent to UT Health Quitman

  • Additional partners

    Foundation partners, community organizations, and corporate sponsors to be added as confirmed.

Project Progress

Where we are right now.

From a 2020 kitchen-table conversation to a federally backed regional project, the Memory Health Life Center is being built in plain view.

  1. 2020

    Vision sparked

    Annette Simpkins and Orval Lindsey pitch the memory-care idea to Quitman Mayor Randy Dunn.

    Complete

  2. 2023 – 24

    Community campaign

    Over $1M raised from Wood County residents and supporters.

    In progress

  3. 2024

    $6M federal earmark

    Federal funds awarded for the project, contingent on a Texas state match.

    Awarded

  4. 2025

    State match advocacy

    The Texas legislative session is the critical window to unlock federal funds with a state match.

    Status pending

  5. Next

    Design & ground-breaking

    Design finalization, permitting, and the path to construction on the 29-acre Quitman campus.

    Upcoming
See the full timeline Read the latest update →

Families & Caregivers

If you're caring for someone with dementia, we see you.

The Memory Health Life Center is being built for families like yours. While we're building, we're here with resources, events, and a place to stay informed.

Resources

Curated guides on dementia, daily care, safety, and planning ahead.

Events

Caregiver workshops, community conversations, and educational programs.

Endorsements

Leaders standing with us.

Federal and state officials, the UT System, regional economic leaders, and our own foundation are on record in support of the Memory Health Life Center.

Additional letters from Sen. Bryan Hughes, Rep. Cole Hefner, Dr. Brigham C. Willis (UT Tyler SOM), Dr. Kirk A. Calhoun (UT Health East Texas), Dr. Sue Cox (UT Austin Dell Medical School), and the Alzheimer's Association are on file with the foundation.
